description: Fix two confusing errors in the start command (misleading version mismatch fix guidance; port conflict doesn't detect own running server) and fix the tool command's unhelpful error output (dumps 100 tool names on typo; disabled-tool message says 'Ask the user' when a human is running the CLI).

userStories.title: Fix version mismatch warning and port conflict message in start command
userStories.description: Two separate issues in platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts: (1) The version mismatch warning always says 'Run: npm install -g @opentabs-dev/cli@latest' even when CLI is newer than the bundled server — the fix should say 'Restart the server to use the updated version.' (2) The port-in-use error for custom ports (--port XXXX) says 'Use a different port' without checking if OpenTabs itself is running — add a health check so if the port serves OpenTabs, the message tells the user to run opentabs stop instead.
acceptanceCriteria: When CLI version > server version, printVersionWarning shows 'Restart the server to update it to the latest version.' instead of 'Run: npm install -g @opentabs-dev/cli@latest'
When CLI version < server version, printVersionWarning still shows the 'npm install -g @opentabs-dev/cli@latest' message

When port is in use by an OpenTabs server (health check returns x-opentabs-version header), the port conflict error says 'OpenTabs is already running on port X. Run: opentabs stop --port X' with the correct port number
When port is in use by a non-OpenTabs process, the port conflict error retains the current 'Use a different port' message
The health check is non-fatal with a short timeout (1-2s) — if it fails/times out, fall through to the existing 'port in use' message

userStories.title: Fix tool schema not-found and disabled-tool error messages
userStories.description: Two issues in platform/cli/src/commands/tool.ts: (1) When a tool name is not found, the code dumps a sorted list of all 100 available tools — replace with a short message pointing to opentabs tool list. (2) The disabled tool response says 'Ask the user to enable it in the OpenTabs side panel' — when a human runs opentabs tool call directly, this message is nonsensical. The server-side message in mcp-tool-dispatch.ts should include CLI-accessible fix guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs tool schema nonexistent_tool prints 'Tool "nonexistent_tool" not found.' followed by 'Run opentabs tool list to see all available tools.' — no 100-item list

opentabs tool call <disabled-browser-tool> shows a response that includes guidance to enable the tool via opentabs config set (e.g., 'opentabs config set plugin-permission.browser-tool auto') in addition to the side panel mention
opentabs tool list output is unchanged
